name: Atlassian MCP Server (Community)
what_it_does: Atlassian MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Jira and Confluence — querying and creating Jira issues, searching Confluence pages, managing sprint boards, updating issue status, and integrating Atlassian's project management and knowledge base platforms into agent-driven development and team productivity workflows.
use_cases: Querying and updating Jira tickets from development workflow agents
Searching Confluence for documentation from knowledge retrieval agents
Creating and triaging Jira issues from incident response agents
Summarizing sprint status from project management agents
Generating standup reports from team productivity agents
Integrating Jira updates into coding workflow from development agents
not_for: Atlassian Cloud users who want official integration (use Atlassian's official MCP when available)
Teams without Jira or Confluence (use Linear, Notion, or GitHub MCPs)
Read-only reporting only — this has write access that requires care
best_when: A development team using Jira and Confluence needs AI agents to query project status, create issues, and search documentation as part of their workflow.
avoid_when: You use Linear, GitHub Issues, or other project tools — or need official Atlassian MCP with enterprise support.

auth.notes: Atlassian API token required (not password). Create at id.atlassian.com. Cloud and Server/Data Center URLs required. Jira and Confluence may need separate tokens.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Jira/Confluence: free tier for up to 10 users
pricing.notes: Atlassian Cloud has a free tier. Community MCP server is free. Enterprise Atlassian plans required for advanced features.

performance.rate_limits: Atlassian API: rate limits per plan tier; Standard limits documented

known_agent_gotchas: Jira API field names are complex — 'issuetype', 'customfield_XXXXX' — learn your instance's schema
Atlassian Cloud vs Server/Data Center have different API versions and endpoints
Write operations (issue creation, status transitions) affect live project tracking — verify before executing
Community MCP — not official Atlassian; Atlassian REST API changes may break it
JQL (Jira Query Language) syntax required for advanced issue searches
Confluence page IDs needed for specific page operations — not always easy to discover
agent_readiness.security_notes: Atlassian API token — not password. Store as environment variable. HTTPS enforced by Atlassian. Community MCP. Project management data may be sensitive — use minimum required scopes.

security_score_components.security_notes: API token auth. HTTPS enforced. Community MCP. Project data is sensitive — minimum required access.
